ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on anchor elements
ai.description: The selector `a` within the grouped rule removes the default browser focus outline. No global or specific replacement focus indicator (box-shadow, border, or outline) is defined for anchor elements, leaving keyboard users unable to see when links have focus.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on input elements
ai.description: The selector `input:focus` removes the outline and sets border-color to #ddd, which is identical to the default border-color, resulting in no visible focus change for keyboard users.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on select elements
ai.description: The selector `select:focus` removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus indicator (box-shadow, border, or outline), making it impossible for keyboard users to track focus.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on textarea elements
ai.description: The selector `textarea:focus` removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus indicator, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on CTA button
ai.description: The selector `#cta-btn:focus` explicitly sets outline to none without providing a replacement visual indicator such as box-shadow, border, or outline, leaving keyboard users without focus visibility.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on gallery images
ai.description: The selector `.gallery-grid img:focus` removes the outline for focusable images without providing a visible alternative, preventing keyboard users from identifying which image has focus.

ai.issueType: Insufficient colour contrast on navigation links
ai.description: The colour #0066ff (blue) on #003366 (dark blue) background produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.4:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1) and large text (3:1).

ai.issueType: Insufficient colour contrast on navigation buttons
ai.description: The text colour #0066ff on background #003366 provides insufficient contrast (approximately 2.4:1), failing WCAG AA contrast requirements for interactive elements.

ai.issueType: Insufficient colour contrast on primary CTA button
ai.description: The background colour #ff6b6b (light red) on white text produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.7:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (16px).

ai.issueType: Insufficient colour contrast on form placeholders
ai.description: The placeholder colour #999 on the default white input background produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.8:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for text.

ai.issueType: No forced-colors/high-contrast-mode support
ai.description: The stylesheet sets explicit colours on interactive elements (buttons, links) but contains no `@media (forced-colors: active)` block to ensure visibility in Windows High Contrast mode, potentially rendering controls invisible or unusable.

ai.issueType: Link underline removed with no alternative visual differentiator
ai.description: The selector `header nav a` removes text-decoration without providing a border-bottom, background change, or font-weight increase on hover/focus, relying solely on colour (#0066ff) to distinguish links from surrounding text.

ai.issueType: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
ai.description: The selector 'header nav a' sets 'text-decoration: none', removing the underline from links. Colour alone is used to distinguish them from surrounding text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1.

ai.issueType: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
ai.description: The selector '.hero h2' sets 'letter-spacing: -1px', which condenses characters and reduces readability for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.

ai.issueType: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
ai.description: The selector '#cta-btn' defines a 'transition' property. There is no '@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)' block in the file to disable this motion for users who request it.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #cta-btn:focus
ai.description: The focus state for '#cta-btn' explicitly sets 'outline: none' without providing a box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline as a replacement.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on input:focus
ai.description: The focus state for 'input' sets 'outline: none'. The border-color change to #ddd is identical to the default border, providing no visible focus indicator.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on select:focus
ai.description: The focus state for 'select' sets 'outline: none'. The border-color change to #ddd is identical to the default border, providing no visible focus indicator.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on textarea:focus
ai.description: The focus state for 'textarea' sets 'outline: none'. The border-color change to #ddd is identical to the default border, providing no visible focus indicator.

ai.issueType: Form labels hidden from all users
ai.description: The selector 'label' sets 'display: none', which hides all form labels visually and from screen readers. This makes form inputs inaccessible.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .gallery-grid img:focus
ai.description: The focus state for '.gallery-grid img' sets 'outline: none' without providing a box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline. Images can be focusable if linked.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on section a:focus
ai.description: The focus state for 'section a' sets 'outline: none'. While 'text-decoration: underline' is present, the prompt rules require box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline as a valid replacement for outline removal.

lighthouse.issueType: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
lighthouse.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

lighthouse.issueType: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
lighthouse.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

lighthouse.issueType: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
lighthouse.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

lighthouse.issueType: Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order
lighthouse.description: Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. [Learn more about heading order](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/heading-order).

lighthouse.issueType: Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes
lighthouse.description: Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. [Learn more about the `alt` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/image-alt).

axeCore.description: Ensure the contrast between foreground and background colors meets WCAG 2 AA minimum contrast ratio thresholds
Ensure the contrast between foreground and background colors meets WCAG 2 AA minimum contrast ratio thresholds
Ensure the contrast between foreground and background colors meets WCAG 2 AA minimum contrast ratio thresholds
Ensure the order of headings is semantically correct
Ensure elements have alternative text or a role of none or presentation
